heading: Pumphreys Bridge vs Quairading — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pumphreys Bridge, Western Australia and Quairading, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pumphreys Bridge has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Quairading (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pumphreys Bridge or Quairading safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pumphreys Bridge has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Quairading scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pumphreys Bridge scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Quairading scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pumphreys Bridge or Quairading?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pumphreys Bridge has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
